body:
## Triage Summary

**Type:** Bug | **Confidence:** High | **Reproducibility:** Medium

### Analysis

This is a confirmed error-handling gap in the agent conversation flow. When a backend request returns a 403 Forbidden response with an HTML body (common from reverse proxies and CDNs), the raw HTML is surfaced directly in the agent transcript instead of a user-friendly error message.

### Root Cause

The error originates in `app/src/server/server_api.rs` where `get_public_api_response()` captures the full HTTP response body in `HttpStatusError.body`. For 403 responses, this body is often a full HTML page from the server infrastructure. The code attempts to parse the body as a JSON `ClientError`, but HTML fails deserialization, so the raw body is preserved in the error chain. This error eventually reaches `RenderableAIError::Other` (via `From<&AIApiError>` in `app/src/ai/agent/mod.rs`), which formats it with `{value:?}` — embedding the HTML — and renders it as plain text in the conversation UI (`app/src/ai/blocklist/block/view_impl/common.rs`).

No step in the pipeline strips HTML or maps well-known HTTP status codes (like 401/403) to concise, user-friendly messages.

### Related Files

- `app/src/server/server_api.rs` — error creation with raw HTML body
- `app/src/server/server_api/presigned_upload.rs` — `HttpStatusError` definition
- `app/src/ai/agent/mod.rs` — `RenderableAIError` enum and `From<&AIApiError>` conversion
- `app/src/ai/blocklist/block/view_impl/common.rs` — error rendering in the conversation UI
- `app/src/server/retry_strategies.rs` — transient vs permanent error classification

### Suggested Fix Direction

A fix could operate at one or more of these layers:
1. **`RenderableAIError` classification**: Add a dedicated variant (or extend the existing `From<&AIApiError>` impl) to detect 403/401 status codes and produce a friendly message like the reporter suggested.
2. **`HttpStatusError` body sanitization**: Before storing the body, detect HTML content (e.g., check for `<!doctype` or `text/html` content type) and replace it with a generic status-code-based message.
3. **Rendering layer**: In `render_failed_output`, sanitize or truncate error messages that appear to contain HTML markup.

performed_via_github_app.name: GitHub Actions
performed_via_github_app.description: Automate your workflow from idea to production

body: @oz-agent I'd expect this to auto-apply a `ready-to-spec` label, since the design requirements are clearly defined.
